disease.name: Dyslipidemia and Hyperlipidemia
disease.note: Dyslipidemia is a major modifiable risk factor for ASCVD. The ACC-AHA 2018 guideline uses the Pooled Cohort Equations (PCE) for 10-year ASCVD risk estimation to guide statin therapy decisions. LDL-C is the primary treatment target. High-intensity statins (atorvastatin 40-80mg, rosuvastatin 20-40mg) reduce LDL-C by >=50%. Ezetimibe and PCSK9 inhibitors (evolocumab, alirocumab) are add-on therapies. Familial hypercholesterolemia requires early identification and aggressive treatment.

severityAssessment.severityLabel: If TG >500 or LDL >190 -> SEVERE; if ASCVD risk >7.5% -> MODERATE; if low risk -> MILD

subtypes.name: Primary Prevention: Statin Eligibility by ASCVD Risk
subtypes.management: Calculate 10-year ASCVD risk using PCE. LDL >=190 or DM age 40-75: high-intensity statin (atorvastatin 40-80mg or rosuvastatin 20-40mg). LDL 70-189 + risk >=7.5%: moderate-to-high intensity statin. Risk 5-7.5%: discuss, consider moderate intensity. Risk <5%: lifestyle modification. Reassess lipids 4-12 weeks after initiation.

subtypes.name: Familial Hypercholesterolemia (HeFH and HoFH)
subtypes.management: High-intensity statin + ezetimibe. If LDL not at goal (>50% reduction or <100), add PCSK9 inhibitor (evolocumab 420mg monthly or alirocumab 75-150mg q2wk). Cascade screening of first-degree relatives. HoFH: triple therapy (statin + ezetimibe + PCSK9i), consider lomitapide or evinacumab. LDL apheresis if refractory. Echocardiogram to assess aortic valve.

subtypes.name: Severe Hypertriglyceridemia (TG >=500 mg/dL)
subtypes.management: Address secondary causes. Fibrate first line: fenofibrate 145mg daily. If mixed dyslipidemia, use fenofibrate with moderate statin (avoid gemfibrozil + statin). Omega-3 fatty acids EPA 4g/day (icosapent ethyl) reduce CV events. Lifestyle: very low fat diet (<15% calories), no alcohol, strict DM control. Goal TG <500 to prevent pancreatitis.

subtypes.name: Statin Intolerance and Alternative Therapy
subtypes.management: Confirm true intolerance: dechallenge (stop 2-4 weeks) and rechallenge (at least 2 attempts). Try alternate statin (low dose rosuvastatin 2.5-5mg or pitavastatin 2mg). Reduce dose or alternate day dosing. Add ezetimibe 10mg (LDL reduction ~15-20%). PCSK9 inhibitors for high risk if statin cannot achieve goal. Bempedoic acid 180mg as additional option.

bedside.name: Fasting lipid panel (12h fast, water only)

laboratory.name: Total cholesterol, LDL-C calculated or direct, HDL-C, triglycerides
Lipoprotein(a)
laboratory.indication: once in lifetime for ASCVD risk refinement

laboratory.name: CK baseline before statin if muscle symptoms concern
laboratory.indication: baseline if high risk for myopathy

laboratory.name: Fasting glucose or HbA1c
laboratory.indication: statin increases diabetes risk, baseline needed

management.protocolReference: ACC-AHA 2018 Guideline on Management of Blood Cholesterol, ESC/EAS 2019 Dyslipidemia Guidelines, NLA Recommendations

pathways.criteria: LDL <190, age 40-75, no DM, ASCVD risk <5%
pathways.treatment: Lifestyle modification: TLC diet, saturated fat <7% calories, dietary cholesterol <200mg/day, plant sterols 2g/day, viscous fiber 10-25g/day. Exercise 150min/week. Recheck lipids in 4-12 weeks. No pharmacotherapy unless risk discussion supports it.

pathways.criteria: LDL >=190, or DM 40-75, or ASCVD risk >=7.5% with LDL 70-189
pathways.treatment: High-intensity statin: atorvastatin 40-80mg or rosuvastatin 20-40mg. Target >=50% LDL reduction. If not at target, add ezetimibe 10mg. If still not at target, consider PCSK9i. Recheck lipids 4-12 weeks after initiation, then q3-12 months once stable.

pathways.criteria: established ASCVD (prior MI, stroke, PAD, revascularization)
pathways.treatment: High-intensity statin on day 1 of admission. Target LDL <70 or <55 for very high risk (polyvascular disease, recurrent events). Add ezetimibe 10mg if not at goal. Add PCSK9i if LDL remains >=70 on statin + ezetimibe. Check Lp(a) once.
